metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the ticket description is injected into the scope-intent overlay's prompt context without reusing or overloading the existing ticket_context variable (which is the diff in code-review), naming the variable name and the wiring path (e.g., a new step input, a separate prompt variable).
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 states 'base and other overlays remain ticket-blind' but in the code-review gate ticket_context IS the diff context for every overlay (CodeReviewBatchRunner line 58 injects `ticket_context=self._context` where _context is the assembled diff). The new overlay needs the TICKET DESCRIPTION injected as a separate variable — the plan does not name how ticket description reaches the new overlay's prompt without contaminating other overlays' ticket_context.
findings.location: What section: 'ONLY this overlay sees ticket context; base + all other overlays stay ticket-blind'; AC3: 'Base and other overlays remain ticket-blind; overlay is advisory.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the 'content-trigger seam' already exists in production code (grep `content_trigger` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/`); if it doesn't, the plan must either explicitly own building it (and name this as net-new scope) or reference the ticket that ships it as a prerequisite.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it 'reuses the content-trigger seam' as if this seam already exists in the codebase, but grepping the production code finds only glob_triggered_overlays (file-path matching) in registry.py — no content-trigger mechanism exists. If the seam must be built here, this plan introduces a new generalized abstraction with exactly one consumer (this overlay), violating the Rule-of-Three and potentially re-building something another in-flight ticket also intends to build.
findings.location: What section: 'reuses the content-trigger seam'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Prerequisites` section naming the ticket that ships the content-trigger seam, OR rename 'reuses' to 'introduces' and scope the seam's implementation explicitly, citing the single current consumer and acknowledging Rule-of-Three deferral.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether a 'content-trigger seam' (non-glob, predicate-based overlay dispatch) exists or needs to be built; if it must be built, add it to scope and describe the dispatch contract explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it 'reuses the content-trigger seam' for ticket-based overlay firing, but no such seam exists in the codebase. The only trigger mechanism is `glob_triggered_overlays` (file-path glob matching in `registry.py:245`). There is no `content_trigger`, `content_triggered_overlay`, or analogous predicate-based dispatch. The plan's entire wiring strategy depends on a capability that does not yet exist and must be built — but the plan treats it as pre-existing infrastructure.
findings.location: What section: 'New scope-intent overlay, content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket: trailer (reuses the content-trigger seam)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'reuses the content-trigger seam' with a specification of the actual new trigger mechanism needed (e.g., a ticket-presence predicate in registry.py / overlay_union / code-review.yaml) since no such seam exists today.
findings.finding: The plan claims to 'reuse the content-trigger seam' for ticket-based overlay firing, but no such seam exists in the codebase. The sole overlay-trigger mechanism is glob_triggered_overlays (file-path glob matching in registry.py). There is no content-trigger abstraction to reuse; the plan would need to CREATE one.
findings.location: What section: 'content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket: trailer (reuses the content-trigger seam)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for reading the commit message and resolving ticket refs within the code-review gate's data flow (e.g. a new scripted `uses:` op or an extension to `assemble_diff`), replacing the reference to a 'content-trigger seam' that does not exist in this gate.
findings.finding: The plan's trigger mechanism references a 'content-trigger seam' that does not exist in the code-review gate. The code-review gate has two trigger mechanisms: file-path glob matching (`overlay_union`/`glob_triggered_overlays`) and LLM-routed `recommend_overlays`. The plan-review `overlay_triggers` step (keyword/structural) is not wired into the code-review gate at all. An implementer who looks for this seam will find nothing to reuse and must invent a new mechanism — or the plan intends something else that isn't specified. Additionally, the described trigger requires reading the commit message (to call `extract_ticket_refs`), but `assemble_diff_context` only reads `git diff`; there is no commit-message read step in the current gate data flow.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — 'reuses the content-trigger seam'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scripted `uses: assemble_ticket_scope` op (analogous to `assemble_diff`) to code-review.yaml that: (1) reads `git log -1 --format=%B HEAD` (or accepts `commit_message` as an explicit input for testability), (2) calls `extract_ticket_refs`, (3) resolves ALL extracted candidates via `resolve_ticket_id` (not just the first, since `verify_commit_message` short-circuits), (4) emits `has_ticket_refs: bool` + `ticket_contexts: [...]`. The scope-intent overlay's `when:` guard references `steps.assemble_ticket_scope.outputs.has_ticket_refs`, exactly matching the existing gate's `when:` pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or design the actual trigger seam: document how the commit trailer is read (new workflow step?), how the resolved-ticket flag reaches `overlay_union` or a new trigger step, and confirm whether any existing hook can be reused — or explicitly acknowledge this is a net-new mechanism in the workflow.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it 'reuses the content-trigger seam' but no such named seam exists in the code-review gate; the only overlay-trigger mechanisms are file-glob `applies_to` matching and base-reviewer `recommend_overlays` in `overlay_union`. A ticket-resolution-based conditional trigger is a net-new mechanism, not a reuse.
findings.location: ## What — 'content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket: trailer (reuses the content-trigger seam)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new `assemble_ticket_refs` workflow step after `assemble_diff` that calls `extract_ticket_refs` + resolves all candidates, emits `{ticket_ids: [...], has_ticket: bool}`, and passes `has_ticket` as the `when:` guard on the scope-intent overlay entry in the batch criteria list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the 'content-trigger seam' already exists or must be built as part of this plan; if it is a dependency on the deletion-impact plan, state that ordering constraint explicitly and verify the seam's interface before committing to 'reuse'.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it 'reuses the content-trigger seam' but no such seam exists in the codebase. The only trigger mechanism is `glob_triggered_overlays` (file-path matching in `registry.py`). The content-trigger seam is a proposed-but-unshipped feature from a sibling plan; treating it as an existing reusable primitive is a capability gap.
findings.location: What section — 'content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket trailer (reuses the content-trigger seam)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the commit message / rebar-ticket trailer reaches the code-review gate at runtime (e.g. a new gate input, a new `assemble_diff` output, or a dedicated step), and name the wiring changes required in `code-review.yaml` and `assemble_diff`.
findings.finding: The plan requires reading the commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer at diff-review time, but `assemble_diff` (the entry-point for diff context assembly) reads only the git diff and changed-file list — it has no code path to read the commit message or extract trailer refs. 'Ticket-context assembly from the trailer' is a new, unbuilt capability.
findings.location: Scope — 'trigger wiring, ticket-context assembly from the trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and spec the new content-trigger seam (commit-message trailer check at gate entry): where it lives (new scripted step? gate_dispatch hook?), its interface contract with the workflow executor, and how it gates the new overlay — rather than claiming to reuse a non-existent seam.
findings.finding: The plan says it 'reuses the content-trigger seam' but no such seam exists anywhere in the codebase. The only trigger mechanisms are `glob_triggered_overlays` (file-path glob matching in `registry.py`) and the base reviewer's `recommend_overlays` escalation signal. There is no content-based (diff-text or commit-message) trigger hook to reuse.
findings.location: Plan § What / § Scope — 'reuses the content-trigger seam'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the ticket-context assembly protocol: which new workflow step/scripted op reads the commit message, calls extract_ticket_refs, resolves each ref, unions ticket bodies, and injects the result — and how it threads to only the scope-intent overlay without contaminating existing overlays.
findings.finding: The ticket-context assembly protocol is unspecified. The plan says the overlay assembles 'ticket-context from the trailer' and checks the diff against the union of resolved tickets, but the code-review gate currently has no path that (a) reads the commit message, (b) calls extract_ticket_refs, (c) resolves multiple ticket ids, or (d) injects ticket content into a single overlay while keeping others ticket-blind. The WHERE and HOW are entirely absent.
findings.location: Plan § What — 'ticket-context assembly from the trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the scope-intent overlay's criterion vocabulary for the diff context: specify the overlay id to add to OVERLAY_IDS, the named dimension(s) the overlay emits (e.g. 'scope-drift', 'undocumented-scope'), the checklist keys, and the criteria_routing.json entry shape — distinct from plan-review's ticket-structural E3/F4/G5.
findings.finding: The 'scope-intent criterion vocabulary' for the new code-review overlay is never defined. The plan says it 'reuses plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary,' but plan-review's scope-intent criteria (E3 intent-fidelity, F4 user/value, G5 decomposition) are ticket-structural rubrics not applicable to a diff. No code-review analog criterion id, checklist, or output dimension is specified. The closed OVERLAY_IDS tuple in registry.py has no 'scope-intent' entry and no slot for the new overlay.
findings.location: Plan § What — 'Reuses plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary'; Plan § Scope — 'criteria_routing.json entry'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that validates the true-positive case (a diff that intentionally drifts from the referenced ticket's stated scope is flagged by the overlay) and one AC for the graceful-skip/error behavior when the trailer ticket cannot be resolved.
findings.finding: The acceptance criteria cover only the false-positive (no-spurious-finding) path for the new overlay but omit any criterion that the overlay actually detects a genuine scope-drift case (the true-positive path) and any criterion for the error/empty path when the trailer ticket cannot be resolved — making the AC set happy-path-only for a new user-facing behavior.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC2, AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Overlay produces a scope-drift advisory when the diff introduces changes clearly outside the referenced ticket's described scope (eval with a synthetic out-of-scope diff).' Add: 'When the trailer ticket ID cannot be resolved, the overlay skips gracefully and emits no finding rather than erroring.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` (specifically the `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple) to the Scope, since the closed overlay-id enum must be extended for the new overlay to be valid.
findings.finding: The plan states 'criteria_routing.json entry' as a scoped deliverable, implying the new overlay id (scope-intent or similar) can be added to that file alone. But `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` maintains a CLOSED `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple (lines 34-46) that is the single source of truth for valid overlay ids — unknown ids are silently dropped by `filter_recommend_overlays`. The new overlay id must be added to `OVERLAY_IDS` too, or the overlay can never be escalation-triggered. The plan does not mention this closed enum.
findings.location: Scope section: 'criteria_routing.json entry'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `registry.py` (`OVERLAY_IDS` extension) to the Scope bullet list alongside `criteria_routing.json entry` and `trigger wiring`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS), src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml (batch criteria list for both round_a and round_b), and src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md (overlay catalog) to the Scope as required edit targets for the new 'scope-intent' overlay.
findings.finding: 'scope-intent' is absent from the closed OVERLAY_IDS tuple in src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py. Every dispatch path reads exclusively from this tuple: glob_triggered_overlays, overlay_union, overlay_flag_key, the batch criteria 'when:' expressions in code-review.yaml, and the no-drift test (test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py) all enumerate OVERLAY_IDS. The plan's Scope lists only the prompt file, trigger wiring, and a criteria_routing.json entry — it omits registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS), code-review.yaml (batch step criteria list), and code-review-base.md (overlay catalog list) as required edit targets.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md, trigger wiring, ticket-context assembly from the trailer; criteria_routing.json entry'
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'scope-intent' to OVERLAY_IDS in registry.py; add a `{ prompt: code-review-scope-intent, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_scope_intent }}" }` entry to both round_a and round_b in code-review.yaml; add the overlay to code-review-base.md's catalog list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'scope-intent'` to the `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` (and to the corresponding gate YAML `round_a`/`round_b` batch criteria lists) as a named Scope deliverable.
findings.finding: The new overlay id `scope-intent` (implied by the new file `code-review-scope-intent.md`) is absent from the closed `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py`. Every dispatch path (`glob_triggered_overlays`, `overlay_union`, `filter_recommend_overlays`) iterates over or validates against this tuple; an id not in it is silently dropped. The plan's Scope lists `criteria_routing.json entry` but omits adding the id to `OVERLAY_IDS`.
findings.location: Scope — 'src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md, trigger wiring, ticket-context assembly from the trailer; criteria_routing.json entry'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"scope-intent"` to the `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple in `registry.py`, and add a `when:` entry for `code-review-scope-intent` in both the `round_a` and `round_b` batch blocks of `code-review.yaml`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the ticket-blindness invariant (only the scope-intent overlay receives ticket context) so future overlay authors can discover and respect this contract.
findings.finding: The ticket-blindness invariant — 'ONLY this overlay sees ticket context; base + all other overlays stay ticket-blind' — is a new, non-trivial architectural contract that future overlay authors must respect, but the plan includes no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to capture it.
findings.location: What section: 'ONLY this overlay sees ticket context; base + all other overlays stay ticket-blind (neutrality).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (or a named section in CLAUDE.md / a design-doc) titled e.g. 'Overlay ticket-context isolation' stating: only the scope-intent overlay receives ticket context; all other overlays must remain ticket-blind to preserve neutrality. Reference it from criteria_routing.json or the overlay directory README.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '(eval)' in AC2 with a specific, named evaluation method — either a deterministic fixture (e.g., a synthetic two-ticket commit with known diff) and an assertion command, or an explicit reference to the existing eval harness and its pass criterion.
findings.finding: AC2's verification method '(eval)' is undefined — an executor cannot determine what artifact, command, or dataset constitutes a passing evaluation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Example: 'A multi-ticket commit referencing TICKET-1 and TICKET-2 whose diff implements both produces zero scope-drift findings; verified by running `rebar eval tests/fixtures/multi_ticket_scope` and asserting findings == [].'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback specification for ticket resolution failure: define the behavior when the store is unavailable (infra error), when no refs resolve (gate skips, logs coverage gap), and when only a subset of multi-ticket refs resolve (fire on partial set vs. skip entirely).
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for ticket resolution failure scenarios: when none of the trailer refs resolve (store unavailable, infra error, aliases not found), when the store is not mounted (exit-2 scenario in verify_commit.py), or when a multi-ticket commit has only partial resolution. The plan's AC only covers the happy path ('fires only when a ticket resolves') but defines no behavior for the failure/partial cases.
findings.location: Plan § Acceptance Criteria — AC1 'Overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: store unavailable or zero refs resolve → overlay silently skips and records a coverage-gap note (consistent with base_failure_result pattern in registry.py); partial resolution → fire on the resolved subset only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or test fixture for each AC (e.g., a CLI invocation against a synthetic commit with/without a trailer, a grep of the assembled prompt, or a named eval fixture) so that done-ness produces observable evidence.
findings.finding: No AC has a concrete proving command — the plan describes what to build but not how to verify each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all three ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: For AC1: `rebar review <commit-with-trailer>` emits scope-intent overlay output; `rebar review <commit-without-trailer>` does not. For AC3: assert that the base-overlay prompt assembled for the same commit contains no ticket text (inspect logged prompt or add a unit test). For AC2: name an eval fixture (e.g., `tests/fixtures/multi_ticket_commit`) and assert zero scope-drift findings against it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC or plan text specifying overlay behavior for non-happy-path states: ticket resolution failure, unreachable ticket backend, empty/malformed trailer refs, and partial ticket data (e.g., 'overlay skips and emits no finding if ticket context cannot be fully resolved').
findings.finding: The plan specifies only the happy path (ticket resolves → overlay fires → checks diff) and one true-positive scenario (multi-ticket commit), but does not address what happens when ticket resolution fails, ticket content is unavailable/malformed, or the extract_ticket_refs call returns empty or errors — leaving the non-happy-path behavior of this user-facing advisory overlay unspecified.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'If ticket resolution fails or returns no usable context, the overlay emits no finding and logs a debug-level warning rather than erroring or producing a spurious scope finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the implementation will call `extract_ticket_refs` + resolve ALL candidates (not `verify_commit_message` which short-circuits on the first hit), and document this as a new integration path in scope.
findings.finding: The plan states it scopes against 'the UNION of all referenced tickets', but the existing `verify_commit_message` seam it implicitly relies on returns only the FIRST resolving candidate — obtaining the full union requires calling `extract_ticket_refs` directly and resolving every candidate, which is a distinct code path not currently exposed as a reusable seam.
findings.location: ## What — 'Resolves the FULL ticket set via extract_ticket_refs (verify_commit.py supports multiple ids: multiple trailer lines AND whitespace-separated tokens)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one observability artifact (a structured log line or metric counter) for each new failure path: ticket-not-resolved, ticket-lookup-error, and overlay-LLM-call-failure, so operators can distinguish no-issue from silent-error.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new failure points — ticket resolution and the LLM call for this overlay — but specifies no observability instrumentation (log lines, metrics, or traces) to let operators diagnose why the overlay fired, didn't fire, or errored.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria and Scope sections; no observability artifact listed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify 'ticket-blind' relative to the existing `ticket_context` variable (currently = assembled diff, not rebar ticket); specify the new variable name the scope-intent overlay will use for rebar-ticket content so it doesn't collide with the existing injection.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'base + all other overlays stay ticket-blind (neutrality)' but in the current codebase every reviewer — base and all 11 specialist overlays — already receives `{{ticket_context}}` as an injected input (code-review.yaml lines 64, 133, 158; code-review-base.md line 68; all overlay .md files). The field named `ticket_context` currently carries the assembled diff context, not a ticket. The plan's 'ticket-blind' claim is unverifiable as stated because it conflates the existing `ticket_context` variable name (which carries diff content today) with the rebar-ticket trailer context the plan proposes injecting.
findings.location: What section: 'ONLY this overlay sees ticket context; base + all other overlays stay ticket-blind (neutrality)'
findings.suggested_fix: Name the new variable `rebar_ticket_context` (or similar) in the overlay prompt and gate YAML step, and state explicitly that the existing `ticket_context` (diff content) remains unchanged in all other steps.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
